---
title: "Graph Patterns"
subtitle: "A list of common CIDOC CRM graph patterns"
---

This page lists common graph patterns that we encounter when modelling data in [LINCS](https://lincsproject.ca) using the [CIDOC CRM](https://cidoc-crm.org) ontology. When modelling data, we select appropriate graph patterns and combine them together to create our data model. Then, we apply the model to our data, resulting in a knowledge graph.

## General Patterns

### Names & Titles

An entity has a name (or title).

![Caption](./images/entity-name.svg)

### Type

An entity has a type.

![Caption](./images/entity-type.svg)

## Patterns Involving Event

The following patterns are applicable to any event or its subclasses (colored in blue), such as Birth, Death, Occupation, Production...

### Event Date

An event took place on a certain date.

### Event Place

An event took place at a certain place.

![Caption](./images/event-place.svg)

## Patterns Involving People

The following patterns are applicable to any person.

### Birth Date

A person was born on a certain date.

### Birth Place

A person was born in a certain place.

![Caption](./images/birth-place.svg)

### Death Date

A person died on a certain date.

### Death Place

![Caption](./images/Death_Location.png)

### Occupation

A person had an occupation such as an employment or appointment.

## Patterns Involving Groups

### Occupation

A group has a member (a person).

## Patterns Involving Places



### Other useful commands
